2331 // Remember how many variables are in the list before we search.
2332 const uint32_t original_size = variables.GetSize();
2333
2334 llvm::StringRef basename;
2335 llvm::StringRef context;
2336 bool name_is_mangled = Mangled::GetManglingScheme(name.GetStringRef()) !=
2337 Mangled::eManglingSchemeNone;
2338
2339 if (!CPlusPlusLanguage::ExtractContextAndIdentifier(name.GetStringRef(),
2340 context, basename))
2341 basename = name.GetStringRef();
2342
2343 // Loop invariant: Variables up to this index have been checked for context
2344 // matches.
2345 uint32_t pruned_idx = original_size;
2346
2347 SymbolContext sc;
2348 m_index->GetGlobalVariables(ConstString(basename), [&](DWARFDIE die) {
2349 if (!sc.module_sp)
2350 sc.module_sp = m_objfile_sp->GetModule();
2351 assert(sc.module_sp);
2352
2353 if (die.Tag() != DW_TAG_variable && die.Tag() != DW_TAG_member)
2354 return IterationAction::Continue;
2355
2356 auto *dwarf_cu = llvm::dyn_cast<DWARFCompileUnit>(die.GetCU());
2357 if (!dwarf_cu)
2358 return IterationAction::Continue;
2359 sc.comp_unit = GetCompUnitForDWARFCompUnit(*dwarf_cu);
2360
2361 if (parent_decl_ctx) {
2362 if (DWARFASTParser *dwarf_ast = GetDWARFParser(*die.GetCU())) {
2363 CompilerDeclContext actual_parent_decl_ctx =
2364 dwarf_ast->GetDeclContextContainingUIDFromDWARF(die);
2365
2366 /// If the actual namespace is inline (i.e., had a DW_AT_export_symbols)
2367 /// and a child (possibly through other layers of inline namespaces)
2368 /// of the namespace referred to by 'basename', allow the lookup to
2369 /// succeed.
2370 if (!actual_parent_decl_ctx ||
2371 (actual_parent_decl_ctx != parent_decl_ctx &&
2372 !parent_decl_ctx.IsContainedInLookup(actual_parent_decl_ctx)))
2373 return IterationAction::Continue;
2374 }
2375 }
2376
2377 ParseAndAppendGlobalVariable(sc, die, variables);
2378 while (pruned_idx < variables.GetSize()) {
2379 VariableSP var_sp = variables.GetVariableAtIndex(pruned_idx);
2380 if (name_is_mangled ||
2381 var_sp->GetName().GetStringRef().contains(name.GetStringRef()))
2382 ++pruned_idx;
2383 else
2384 variables.RemoveVariableAtIndex(pruned_idx);
2385 }
2386
2387 if (variables.GetSize() - original_size < max_matches)
2388 return IterationAction::Continue;
2389
2390 return IterationAction::Stop;
2391 });
2392
2393 // Return the number of variable that were appended to the list
2394 const uint32_t num_matches = variables.GetSize() - original_size;
2395 if (log && num_matches > 0) {
2396 GetObjectFile()->GetModule()->LogMessage(
2397 log,
2398 "SymbolFileDWARF::FindGlobalVariables (name=\"{0}\", "
2399 "parent_decl_ctx={1:p}, max_matches={2}, variables) => {3}",
2400 name.GetCString(), static_cast<const void *>(&parent_decl_ctx),
2401 max_matches, num_matches);
2402 }
2403}
2404
2405void SymbolFileDWARF::FindGlobalVariables(const RegularExpression &regex,
2406 uint32_t max_matches,
2407 VariableList &variables) {
2408 std::lock_guard<std::recursive_mutex> guard(GetModuleMutex());
2409 Log *log = GetLog(DWARFLog::Lookups);
2410
2411 if (log) {
2412 GetObjectFile()->GetModule()->LogMessage(
2413 log,
2414 "SymbolFileDWARF::FindGlobalVariables (regex=\"{0}\", "
2415 "max_matches={1}, variables)",
2416 regex.GetText().str().c_str(), max_matches);
2417 }
2418
2419 // Remember how many variables are in the list before we search.
2420 const uint32_t original_size = variables.GetSize();
2421
2422 SymbolContext sc;
2423 m_index->GetGlobalVariables(regex, [&](DWARFDIE die) {
2424 if (!sc.module_sp)
2425 sc.module_sp = m_objfile_sp->GetModule();
2426 assert(sc.module_sp);
2427
2428 DWARFCompileUnit *dwarf_cu = llvm::dyn_cast<DWARFCompileUnit>(die.GetCU());
2429 if (!dwarf_cu)
2430 return IterationAction::Continue;
2431 sc.comp_unit = GetCompUnitForDWARFCompUnit(*dwarf_cu);
2432
2433 ParseAndAppendGlobalVariable(sc, die, variables);
2434
2435 if (variables.GetSize() - original_size < max_matches)
2436 return IterationAction::Continue;
2437
2438 return IterationAction::Stop;
2439 });
2440}
2441
2442bool SymbolFileDWARF::ResolveFunction(const DWARFDIE &orig_die,
2443 bool include_inlines,
2444 SymbolContextList &sc_list) {
2445 SymbolContext sc;
2446
2447 if (!orig_die)
2448 return false;
2449
2450 // If we were passed a die that is not a function, just return false...
2451 if (!(orig_die.Tag() == DW_TAG_subprogram ||
2452 (include_inlines && orig_die.Tag() == DW_TAG_inlined_subroutine)))
2453 return false;
2454
2455 DWARFDIE die = orig_die;
2456 DWARFDIE inlined_die;
2457 if (die.Tag() == DW_TAG_inlined_subroutine) {
2458 inlined_die = die;
2459
2460 while (true) {
2461 die = die.GetParent();
2462
2463 if (die) {
2464 if (die.Tag() == DW_TAG_subprogram)
2465 break;
2466 } else
2467 break;
2468 }
2469 }
2470 assert(die && die.Tag() == DW_TAG_subprogram);
2471 if (GetFunction(die, sc)) {
2472 // Parse all blocks if needed
2473 if (inlined_die) {
2474 Block &function_block = sc.function->GetBlock(true);
2475 sc.block = function_block.FindBlockByID(inlined_die.GetID());
2476 if (sc.block == nullptr)
2477 sc.block = function_block.FindBlockByID(inlined_die.GetOffset());
2478 }
2479
2480 sc_list.AppendIfUnique(sc, /*merge_symbol_into_function=*/true);
2481 return true;
2482 }
2483
2484 return false;
2485}
2486
2487static llvm::StringRef ClangToItaniumCtorKind(clang::CXXCtorType kind) {
2488 switch (kind) {
2489 case clang::CXXCtorType::Ctor_Complete:
2490 return "C1";
2491 case clang::CXXCtorType::Ctor_Base:
2492 return "C2";
2493 case clang::CXXCtorType::Ctor_Unified:
2494 return "C4";
2495 case clang::CXXCtorType::Ctor_CopyingClosure:
2496 case clang::CXXCtorType::Ctor_DefaultClosure:
2497 case clang::CXXCtorType::Ctor_Comdat:
2498 llvm_unreachable("Unexpected constructor kind.");
2499 }
2500 llvm_unreachable("Fully covered switch above");
2501}
2502
2503static llvm::StringRef ClangToItaniumDtorKind(clang::CXXDtorType kind) {
2504 switch (kind) {
2505 case clang::CXXDtorType::Dtor_Deleting:
2506 return "D0";
2507 case clang::CXXDtorType::Dtor_Complete:
2508 return "D1";
2509 case clang::CXXDtorType::Dtor_Base:
2510 return "D2";
2511 case clang::CXXDtorType::Dtor_Unified:
2512 return "D4";
2513 case clang::CXXDtorType::Dtor_Comdat:
2514 case clang::CXXDtorType::Dtor_VectorDeleting:
2515 llvm_unreachable("Unexpected destructor kind.");
2516 }
2517 llvm_unreachable("Fully covered switch above");
2518}
2519
2520static llvm::StringRef
2521GetItaniumCtorDtorVariant(llvm::StringRef discriminator) {
2522 const bool is_ctor = discriminator.consume_front("C");
2523 if (!is_ctor && !discriminator.consume_front("D"))
2524 return {};
2525
2526 uint64_t structor_kind;
2527 if (!llvm::to_integer(discriminator, structor_kind))
2528 return {};
2529
2530 if (is_ctor) {
2531 if (structor_kind > clang::CXXCtorType::Ctor_Unified)
2532 return {};
2533
2534 return ClangToItaniumCtorKind(
2535 static_cast<clang::CXXCtorType>(structor_kind));
2536 }
2537
2538 if (structor_kind > clang::CXXDtorType::Dtor_Unified)
2539 return {};
2540
2541 return ClangToItaniumDtorKind(static_cast<clang::CXXDtorType>(structor_kind));
2542}
2543
2544llvm::Expected<DWARFDIE>
2545SymbolFileDWARF::FindFunctionDefinition(const FunctionCallLabel &label,
2546 const DWARFDIE &declaration) {
2547 auto do_lookup = [this](llvm::StringRef lookup_name) -> DWARFDIE {
2548 DWARFDIE found;
2549 auto lookup_infos = Module::LookupInfo::MakeLookupInfos(
2550 ConstString(lookup_name), lldb::eFunctionNameTypeFull,
2551 lldb::eLanguageTypeUnknown);
2552
2553 m_index->GetFunctions(lookup_infos, *this, {}, [&](DWARFDIE entry) {
2554 if (entry.GetAttributeValueAsUnsigned(llvm::dwarf::DW_AT_declaration, 0))
2555 return IterationAction::Continue;
2556
2557 found = entry;
2558 return IterationAction::Stop;
2559 });
2560
2561 return found;
2562 };
2563
2564 DWARFDIE definition = do_lookup(label.lookup_name);
2565 if (definition.IsValid())
2566 return definition;
2567
2568 // This is not a structor lookup. Nothing else to be done here.
2569 if (label.discriminator.empty())
2570 return llvm::createStringError(
2571 "no definition DIE found in this SymbolFile");
2572
2573 // We're doing a structor lookup. Maybe we didn't find the structor variant
2574 // because the complete object structor was aliased to the base object
2575 // structor. Try finding the alias instead.
2576 //
2577 // TODO: there are other reasons for why a subprogram definition might be
2578 // missing. Ideally DWARF would tell us more details about which structor
2579 // variant a DIE corresponds to and whether it's an alias.
2580 auto subst_or_err =
2581 CPlusPlusLanguage::SubstituteStructorAliases_ItaniumMangle(
2582 label.lookup_name);
2583 if (!subst_or_err)
2584 return subst_or_err.takeError();
2585
2586 definition = do_lookup(*subst_or_err);
2587
2588 if (!definition.IsValid())
2589 return llvm::createStringError(
2590 "failed to find definition DIE for structor alias in fallback lookup");
2591
2592 return definition;
2593}
2594
2595llvm::Expected<SymbolContext>
2596SymbolFileDWARF::ResolveFunctionCallLabel(FunctionCallLabel &label) {
2597 std::lock_guard<std::recursive_mutex> guard(GetModuleMutex());
2598
2599 if (!label.discriminator.empty()) {
2600 llvm::StringRef from = label.discriminator[0] == 'C' ? "C4" : "D4";
2601
2602 llvm::StringRef variant = GetItaniumCtorDtorVariant(label.discriminator);
2603 if (variant.empty())
2604 return llvm::createStringError(
2605 "failed to get Itanium variant for discriminator");
2606
2607 if (from == variant)
2608 return llvm::createStringError(
2609 "tried substituting unified structor variant into label");
2610
2611 // If we failed to substitute unified mangled name, don't try to do a lookup
2612 // using the unified name because there may be multiple definitions for it
2613 // in the index, and we wouldn't know which one to choose.
2614 auto subst_or_err = CPlusPlusLanguage::SubstituteStructor_ItaniumMangle(
2615 label.lookup_name, from, variant);
2616 if (!subst_or_err)
2617 return llvm::joinErrors(
2618 llvm::createStringErrorV(
2619 "failed to substitute {0} for {1} in mangled name {2}:", from,
2620 variant, label.lookup_name),
2621 subst_or_err.takeError());
2622
2623 if (!*subst_or_err)
2624 return llvm::createStringErrorV(
2625 "got invalid substituted mangled named (substituted "
2626 "{0} for {1} in mangled name {2})",
2627 from, variant, label.lookup_name);
2628
2629 label.lookup_name = subst_or_err->GetStringRef();
2630 }
2631
2632 DWARFDIE die = GetDIE(label.symbol_id);
2633 if (!die.IsValid())
2634 return llvm::createStringErrorV("invalid DIE ID in {0}", label);
2635
2636 // Label was created using a declaration DIE. Need to fetch the definition
2637 // to resolve the function call.
2638 if (die.GetAttributeValueAsUnsigned(llvm::dwarf::DW_AT_declaration, 0)) {
2639 auto die_or_err = FindFunctionDefinition(label, die);
2640 if (!die_or_err)
2641 return llvm::joinErrors(
2642 llvm::createStringError("failed to find definition DIE:"),
2643 die_or_err.takeError());
2644
2645 die = std::move(*die_or_err);
2646 }
2647
2648 SymbolContextList sc_list;
2649 if (!ResolveFunction(die, /*include_inlines=*/false, sc_list))
2650 return llvm::createStringError("failed to resolve function");
2651
2652 if (sc_list.IsEmpty())
2653 return llvm::createStringError("failed to find function");
2654
2655 assert(sc_list.GetSize() == 1);
2656
2657 return sc_list[0];
2658}
2659
2660bool SymbolFileDWARF::DIEInDeclContext(const CompilerDeclContext &decl_ctx,
2661 const DWARFDIE &die,
2662 bool only_root_namespaces) {
2663 // If we have no parent decl context to match this DIE matches, and if the
2664 // parent decl context isn't valid, we aren't trying to look for any
2665 // particular decl context so any die matches.
2666 if (!decl_ctx.IsValid()) {
2667 // ...But if we are only checking root decl contexts, confirm that the
2668 // 'die' is a top-level context.
2669 if (only_root_namespaces)
2670 return die.GetParent().Tag() == llvm::dwarf::DW_TAG_compile_unit;
2671
2672 return true;
2673 }
2674
2675 if (die) {
2676 if (DWARFASTParser *dwarf_ast = GetDWARFParser(*die.GetCU())) {
2677 if (CompilerDeclContext actual_decl_ctx =
2678 dwarf_ast->GetDeclContextContainingUIDFromDWARF(die))
2679 return decl_ctx.IsContainedInLookup(actual_decl_ctx);
2680 }
2681 }
2682 return false;
2683}
2684
2685void SymbolFileDWARF::FindFunctions(const Module::LookupInfo &lookup_info,
2686 const CompilerDeclContext &parent_decl_ctx,
2687 bool include_inlines,
2688 SymbolContextList &sc_list) {
2689 std::lock_guard<std::recursive_mutex> guard(GetModuleMutex());
2690 ConstString name = lookup_info.GetLookupName();
2691 FunctionNameType name_type_mask = lookup_info.GetNameTypeMask();
2692
2693 // eFunctionNameTypeAuto should be pre-resolved by a call to
2694 // Module::LookupInfo::LookupInfo()
2695 assert((name_type_mask & eFunctionNameTypeAuto) == 0);
2696
2697 Log *log = GetLog(DWARFLog::Lookups);
2698
2699 if (log) {
2700 GetObjectFile()->GetModule()->LogMessage(
2701 log,
2702 "SymbolFileDWARF::FindFunctions (name=\"{0}\", name_type_mask={1:x}, "
2703 "sc_list)",
2704 name.GetCString(), name_type_mask);
2705 }
2706
2707 if (!DeclContextMatchesThisSymbolFile(parent_decl_ctx))
2708 return;
2709
2710 // If name is empty then we won't find anything.
2711 if (name.IsEmpty())
2712 return;
2713
2714 // Remember how many sc_list are in the list before we search in case we are
2715 // appending the results to a variable list.
2716
2717 const uint32_t original_size = sc_list.GetSize();
2718
2719 llvm::DenseSet<const DWARFDebugInfoEntry *> resolved_dies;
2720
2721 m_index->GetFunctions(lookup_info, *this, parent_decl_ctx, [&](DWARFDIE die) {
2722 if (resolved_dies.insert(die.GetDIE()).second)
2723 ResolveFunction(die, include_inlines, sc_list);
2724 return IterationAction::Continue;
2725 });
2726 // With -gsimple-template-names, a templated type's DW_AT_name will not
2727 // contain the template parameters. Try again stripping '<' and anything
2728 // after, filtering out entries with template parameters that don't match.
2729 {
2730 const llvm::StringRef name_ref = name.GetStringRef();
2731 auto it = name_ref.find('<');
2732 if (it != llvm::StringRef::npos) {
2733 const llvm::StringRef name_no_template_params = name_ref.slice(0, it);
2734
2735 Module::LookupInfo no_tp_lookup_info(
2736 lookup_info, ConstString(name_no_template_params));
2737 m_index->GetFunctions(no_tp_lookup_info, *this, parent_decl_ctx,
2738 [&](DWARFDIE die) {
2739 if (resolved_dies.insert(die.GetDIE()).second)
2740 ResolveFunction(die, include_inlines, sc_list);
2741 return IterationAction::Continue;
2742 });
2743 }
2744 }
2745
2746 // Return the number of variable that were appended to the list
2747 const uint32_t num_matches = sc_list.GetSize() - original_size;
2748
2749 if (log && num_matches > 0) {
2750 GetObjectFile()->GetModule()->LogMessage(
2751 log,
2752 "SymbolFileDWARF::FindFunctions (name=\"{0}\", "
2753 "name_type_mask={1:x}, include_inlines={2:d}, sc_list) => {3}",
2754 name.GetCString(), name_type_mask, include_inlines, num_matches);
2755 }
2756}
2757
2758void SymbolFileDWARF::FindFunctions(const RegularExpression &regex,
2759 bool include_inlines,
2760 SymbolContextList &sc_list) {
2761 std::lock_guard<std::recursive_mutex> guard(GetModuleMutex());
2762 LLDB_SCOPED_TIMERF("SymbolFileDWARF::FindFunctions (regex = '%s')",
2763 regex.GetText().str().c_str());
2764
2765 Log *log = GetLog(DWARFLog::Lookups);
2766
2767 if (log) {
2768 GetObjectFile()->GetModule()->LogMessage(
2769 log, "SymbolFileDWARF::FindFunctions (regex=\"{0}\", sc_list)",
2770 regex.GetText().str().c_str());
2771 }
2772
2773 llvm::DenseSet<const DWARFDebugInfoEntry *> resolved_dies;
2774 m_index->GetFunctions(regex, [&](DWARFDIE die) {
2775 if (resolved_dies.insert(die.GetDIE()).second)
2776 ResolveFunction(die, include_inlines, sc_list);
2777 return IterationAction::Continue;
2778 });
2779}
2780
2781void SymbolFileDWARF::GetMangledNamesForFunction(
2782 const std::string &scope_qualified_name,
2783 std::vector<ConstString> &mangled_names) {
2784 DWARFDebugInfo &info = DebugInfo();
2785 uint32_t num_comp_units = info.GetNumUnits();
2786 for (uint32_t i = 0; i < num_comp_units; i++) {
2787 DWARFUnit *cu = info.GetUnitAtIndex(i);
2788 if (cu == nullptr)
2789 continue;
2790
2791 SymbolFileDWARFDwo *dwo = cu->GetDwoSymbolFile();
2792 if (dwo)
2793 dwo->GetMangledNamesForFunction(scope_qualified_name, mangled_names);
2794 }
2795
2796 for (DIERef die_ref :
2797 m_function_scope_qualified_name_map.lookup(scope_qualified_name)) {
2798 DWARFDIE die = GetDIE(die_ref);
2799 mangled_names.push_back(ConstString(die.GetMangledName()));
2800 }
2801}
2802
2803/// Split a name up into a basename and template parameters.
2804static bool SplitTemplateParams(llvm::StringRef fullname,
2805 llvm::StringRef &basename,
2806 llvm::StringRef &template_params) {
2807 auto it = fullname.find('<');
2808 if (it == llvm::StringRef::npos) {
2809 basename = fullname;
2810 template_params = llvm::StringRef();
2811 return false;
2812 }
2813 basename = fullname.slice(0, it);
2814 template_params = fullname.slice(it, fullname.size());
2815 return true;
2816}
2817
2818static bool UpdateCompilerContextForSimpleTemplateNames(TypeQuery &match) {
2819 // We need to find any names in the context that have template parameters
2820 // and strip them so the context can be matched when -gsimple-template-names
2821 // is being used. Returns true if any of the context items were updated.
2822 bool any_context_updated = false;
2823 for (auto &context : match.GetContextRef()) {
2824 llvm::StringRef basename, params;
2825 if (SplitTemplateParams(context.name.GetStringRef(), basename, params)) {
2826 context.name = ConstString(basename);
2827 any_context_updated = true;
2828 }
2829 }
2830 return any_context_updated;
2831}
2832
2833uint64_t SymbolFileDWARF::GetDebugInfoSize(bool load_all_debug_info) {
2834 DWARFDebugInfo &info = DebugInfo();
2835 uint32_t num_comp_units = info.GetNumUnits();
2836
2837 uint64_t debug_info_size = SymbolFileCommon::GetDebugInfoSize();
2838 // In dwp scenario, debug info == skeleton debug info + dwp debug info.
2839 if (std::shared_ptr<SymbolFileDWARFDwo> dwp_sp = GetDwpSymbolFile())
2840 return debug_info_size + dwp_sp->GetDebugInfoSize();
2841
2842 // In dwo scenario, debug info == skeleton debug info + all dwo debug info.
2843 for (uint32_t i = 0; i < num_comp_units; i++) {
2844 DWARFUnit *cu = info.GetUnitAtIndex(i);
2845 if (cu == nullptr)
2846 continue;
2847
2848 SymbolFileDWARFDwo *dwo = cu->GetDwoSymbolFile(load_all_debug_info);
2849 if (dwo)
2850 debug_info_size += dwo->GetDebugInfoSize();
2851 }
2852 return debug_info_size;
2853}
2854
2855void SymbolFileDWARF::FindTypes(const TypeQuery &query, TypeResults &results) {
2856
2857 // Make sure we haven't already searched this SymbolFile before.
2858 if (results.AlreadySearched(this))
2859 return;
2860
2861 auto type_basename = query.GetTypeBasename();
2862
2863 Log *log = GetLog(DWARFLog::Lookups);
2864 if (log) {
2865 GetObjectFile()->GetModule()->LogMessage(
2866 log, "SymbolFileDWARF::FindTypes(type_basename=\"{0}\")",
2867 type_basename);
2868 }
2869
2870 std::lock_guard<std::recursive_mutex> guard(GetModuleMutex());
2871
2872 TypeQuery query_full(query);
2873 bool have_index_match = false;
2874 m_index->GetTypesWithQuery(query_full, [&](DWARFDIE die) {
2875 if (Type *matching_type = ResolveType(die, true, true)) {
2876 if (!query.GetSearchByMangledName() && matching_type->IsTemplateType()) {
2877 // We have to watch out for case where we lookup a type by basename and
2878 // it matches a template with simple template names. Like looking up
2879 // "Foo" and if we have simple template names then we will match
2880 // "Foo<int>" and "Foo<double>" because all the DWARF has is "Foo" in
2881 // the accelerator tables. The main case we see this in is when the
2882 // expression parser is trying to parse "Foo<int>" and it will first do
2883 // a lookup on just "Foo". We verify the type basename matches before
2884 // inserting the type in the results.
2885 auto CompilerTypeBasename =
2886 matching_type->GetForwardCompilerType().GetTypeName(true);
2887 if (CompilerTypeBasename != query.GetTypeBasename())
2888 return IterationAction::Continue;
2889 }
2890 have_index_match = true;
2891 results.InsertUnique(matching_type->shared_from_this());
2892 }
2893 if (!results.Done(query))
2894 return IterationAction::Continue;
2895
2896 return IterationAction::Stop;
2897 });
2898
2899 if (results.Done(query)) {
2900 if (log) {
2901 GetObjectFile()->GetModule()->LogMessage(
2902 log, "SymbolFileDWARF::FindTypes(type_basename=\"{0}\") => {1}",
2903 type_basename, results.GetTypeMap().GetSize());
2904 }
2905 return;
2906 }
2907
2908 // With -gsimple-template-names, a templated type's DW_AT_name will not
2909 // contain the template parameters. Try again stripping '<' and anything
2910 // after, filtering out entries with template parameters that don't match.
2911 if (!have_index_match && !query.GetSearchByMangledName()) {
2912 // Create a type matcher with a compiler context that is tuned for
2913 // -gsimple-template-names. We will use this for the index lookup and the
2914 // context matching, but will use the original "match" to insert matches
2915 // into if things match. The "match_simple" has a compiler context with
2916 // all template parameters removed to allow the names and context to match.
2917 // The UpdateCompilerContextForSimpleTemplateNames(...) will return true if
2918 // it trims any context items down by removing template parameter names.
2919 TypeQuery query_simple(query);
2920 if (UpdateCompilerContextForSimpleTemplateNames(query_simple)) {
2921 auto type_basename_simple = query_simple.GetTypeBasename();
2922 // Copy our match's context and update the basename we are looking for
2923 // so we can use this only to compare the context correctly.
2924 m_index->GetTypesWithQuery(query_simple, [&](DWARFDIE die) {
2925 std::vector<CompilerContext> qualified_context =
2926 query.GetModuleSearch()
2927 ? die.GetDeclContext(/*derive_template_names=*/true)
2928 : die.GetTypeLookupContext(/*derive_template_names=*/true);
2929 if (query.ContextMatches(qualified_context))
2930 if (Type *matching_type = ResolveType(die, true, true))
2931 results.InsertUnique(matching_type->shared_from_this());
2932 if (!results.Done(query))
2933 return IterationAction::Continue;
2934
2935 return IterationAction::Stop;
2936 });
2937 if (results.Done(query)) {
2938 if (log) {
2939 GetObjectFile()->GetModule()->LogMessage(
2940 log,
2941 "SymbolFileDWARF::FindTypes(type_basename=\"{0}\") => {1} "
2942 "(simplified as \"{2}\")",
2943 type_basename, results.GetTypeMap().GetSize(),
2944 type_basename_simple);
2945 }
2946 return;
2947 }
2948 }
2949 }
2950
2951 // Next search through the reachable Clang modules. This only applies for
2952 // DWARF objects compiled with -gmodules that haven't been processed by
2953 // dsymutil.
2954 UpdateExternalModuleListIfNeeded();
2955
2956 for (const auto &pair : m_external_type_modules) {
2957 if (ModuleSP external_module_sp = pair.second) {
2958 external_module_sp->FindTypes(query, results);
2959 if (results.Done(query)) {
2960 // We don't log the results here as they are already logged in the
2961 // nested FindTypes call
2962 return;
2963 }
2964 }
2965 }
2966}
2967
2968CompilerDeclContext
2969SymbolFileDWARF::FindNamespace(ConstString name,
2970 const CompilerDeclContext &parent_decl_ctx,
2971 bool only_root_namespaces) {
2972 std::lock_guard<std::recursive_mutex> guard(GetModuleMutex());
2973 Log *log = GetLog(DWARFLog::Lookups);
2974
2975 if (log) {
2976 GetObjectFile()->GetModule()->LogMessage(
2977 log, "SymbolFileDWARF::FindNamespace (sc, name=\"{0}\")",
2978 name.GetCString());
2979 }
2980
2981 CompilerDeclContext namespace_decl_ctx;
2982
2983 if (!DeclContextMatchesThisSymbolFile(parent_decl_ctx))
2984 return namespace_decl_ctx;
2985
2986 m_index->GetNamespacesWithParents(name, parent_decl_ctx, [&](DWARFDIE die) {
2987 if (!DIEInDeclContext(parent_decl_ctx, die, only_root_namespaces))
2988 return IterationAction::Continue;
2989
2990 DWARFASTParser *dwarf_ast = GetDWARFParser(*die.GetCU());
2991 if (!dwarf_ast)
2992 return IterationAction::Continue;
2993
2994 namespace_decl_ctx = dwarf_ast->GetDeclContextForUIDFromDWARF(die);
2995 if (namespace_decl_ctx.IsValid())
2996 return IterationAction::Stop;
2997
2998 return IterationAction::Continue;
2999 });
3000
3001 if (log && namespace_decl_ctx) {
3002 GetObjectFile()->GetModule()->LogMessage(
3003 log,
3004 "SymbolFileDWARF::FindNamespace (sc, name=\"{0}\") => "
3005 "CompilerDeclContext({1:p}/{2:p}) \"{3}\"",
3006 name.GetCString(),
3007 static_cast<const void *>(namespace_decl_ctx.GetTypeSystem()),
3008 static_cast<const void *>(namespace_decl_ctx.GetOpaqueDeclContext()),
3009 namespace_decl_ctx.GetName().AsCString("<NULL>"));
3010 }
3011
3012 return namespace_decl_ctx;
3013}
3014
3015TypeSP SymbolFileDWARF::GetTypeForDIE(const DWARFDIE &die,
3016 bool resolve_function_context) {
3017 TypeSP type_sp;
3018 if (die) {
3019 Type *type_ptr = GetDIEToType().lookup(die.GetDIE());
3020 if (type_ptr == nullptr) {
3021 SymbolContextScope *scope;
3022 if (auto *dwarf_cu = llvm::dyn_cast<DWARFCompileUnit>(die.GetCU()))
3023 scope = GetCompUnitForDWARFCompUnit(*dwarf_cu);
3024 else
3025 scope = GetObjectFile()->GetModule().get();
3026 assert(scope);
3027 SymbolContext sc(scope);
3028 const DWARFDebugInfoEntry *parent_die = die.GetParent().GetDIE();
3029 while (parent_die != nullptr) {
3030 if (parent_die->Tag() == DW_TAG_subprogram)
3031 break;
3032 parent_die = parent_die->GetParent();
3033 }
3034 SymbolContext sc_backup = sc;
3035 if (resolve_function_context && parent_die != nullptr &&
3036 !GetFunction(DWARFDIE(die.GetCU(), parent_die), sc))
3037 sc = sc_backup;
3038
3039 type_sp = ParseType(sc, die, nullptr);
3040 } else if (type_ptr != DIE_IS_BEING_PARSED) {
3041 // Get the original shared pointer for this type
3042 type_sp = type_ptr->shared_from_this();
3043 }
3044 }
3045 return type_sp;
3046}
3047
3048DWARFDIE
3049SymbolFileDWARF::GetDeclContextDIEContainingDIE(const DWARFDIE &orig_die) {
3050 if (orig_die) {
3051 DWARFDIE die = orig_die;
3052
3053 while (die) {
3054 // If this is the original DIE that we are searching for a declaration
3055 // for, then don't look in the cache as we don't want our own decl
3056 // context to be our decl context...
3057 if (orig_die != die) {
3058 switch (die.Tag()) {
3059 case DW_TAG_compile_unit:
3060 case DW_TAG_partial_unit:
3061 case DW_TAG_namespace:
3062 case DW_TAG_structure_type:
3063 case DW_TAG_union_type:
3064 case DW_TAG_class_type:
3065 case DW_TAG_lexical_block:
3066 case DW_TAG_subprogram:
3067 return die;
3068 case DW_TAG_inlined_subroutine: {
3069 DWARFDIE abs_die = die.GetReferencedDIE(DW_AT_abstract_origin);
3070 if (abs_die) {
3071 return abs_die;
3072 }
3073 break;
3074 }
3075 default:
3076 break;
3077 }
3078 }
3079
3080 DWARFDIE spec_die = die.GetReferencedDIE(DW_AT_specification);
3081 if (spec_die) {
3082 DWARFDIE decl_ctx_die = GetDeclContextDIEContainingDIE(spec_die);
3083 if (decl_ctx_die)
3084 return decl_ctx_die;
3085 }
3086
3087 DWARFDIE abs_die = die.GetReferencedDIE(DW_AT_abstract_origin);
3088 if (abs_die) {
3089 DWARFDIE decl_ctx_die = GetDeclContextDIEContainingDIE(abs_die);
3090 if (decl_ctx_die)
3091 return decl_ctx_die;
3092 }
3093
3094 die = die.GetParent();
3095 }
3096 }
3097 return DWARFDIE();
3098}
3099
3100Symbol *SymbolFileDWARF::GetObjCClassSymbol(ConstString objc_class_name) {
3101 Symbol *objc_class_symbol = nullptr;
3102 if (m_objfile_sp) {
3103 Symtab *symtab = m_objfile_sp->GetSymtab();
3104 if (symtab) {
3105 objc_class_symbol = symtab->FindFirstSymbolWithNameAndType(
3106 objc_class_name, eSymbolTypeObjCClass, Symtab::eDebugNo,
3107 Symtab::eVisibilityAny);
3108 }
3109 }
3110 return objc_class_symbol;
3111}
3112
3113// This function can be used when a DIE is found that is a forward declaration
3114// DIE and we want to try and find a type that has the complete definition.
3115TypeSP SymbolFileDWARF::FindCompleteObjCDefinitionTypeForDIE(
3116 const DWARFDIE &die, ConstString type_name, bool must_be_implementation) {
3117
3118 TypeSP type_sp;
3119
3120 if (!type_name || (must_be_implementation && !GetObjCClassSymbol(type_name)))
3121 return type_sp;
3122
3123 m_index->GetCompleteObjCClass(
3124 type_name, must_be_implementation, [&](DWARFDIE type_die) {
3125 // Don't try and resolve the DIE we are looking for with the DIE
3126 // itself!
3127 if (type_die == die || !IsStructOrClassTag(type_die.Tag()))
3128 return IterationAction::Continue;
3129
3130 if (must_be_implementation) {
3131 const bool try_resolving_type = type_die.GetAttributeValueAsUnsigned(
3132 DW_AT_APPLE_objc_complete_type, 0);
3133 if (!try_resolving_type)
3134 return IterationAction::Continue;
3135 }
3136
3137 Type *resolved_type = ResolveType(type_die, false, true);
3138 if (!resolved_type || resolved_type == DIE_IS_BEING_PARSED)
3139 return IterationAction::Continue;
3140
3141 DEBUG_PRINTF(
3142 "resolved 0x%8.8" PRIx64 " from %s to 0x%8.8" PRIx64
3143 " (cu 0x%8.8" PRIx64 ")\n",
3144 die.GetID(),
3145 m_objfile_sp->GetFileSpec().GetFilename().AsCString("<Unknown>"),
3146 type_die.GetID(), type_cu->GetID());
3147
3148 if (die)
3149 GetDIEToType()[die.GetDIE()] = resolved_type;
3150 type_sp = resolved_type->shared_from_this();
3151 return IterationAction::Stop;
3152 });
3153 return type_sp;
3154}
3155
3156DWARFDIE
3157SymbolFileDWARF::FindDefinitionDIE(const DWARFDIE &die) {
3158 const char *name = die.GetName();
3159 if (!name)
3160 return {};
3161 if (!die.GetAttributeValueAsUnsigned(DW_AT_declaration, 0))
3162 return die;
3163
3164 Progress progress(llvm::formatv(
3165 "Searching definition DIE in {0}: '{1}'",
3166 GetObjectFile()->GetFileSpec().GetFilename().GetString(), name));
3167
3168 const dw_tag_t tag = die.Tag();
3169
3170 Log *log = GetLog(DWARFLog::TypeCompletion | DWARFLog::Lookups);
3171 if (log) {
3172 GetObjectFile()->GetModule()->LogMessage(
3173 log,
3174 "SymbolFileDWARF::FindDefinitionDIE(tag={0} "
3175 "({1}), name='{2}')",
3176 DW_TAG_value_to_name(tag), tag, name);
3177 }
3178
3179 // Get the type system that we are looking to find a type for. We will
3180 // use this to ensure any matches we find are in a language that this
3181 // type system supports
3182 const LanguageType language = GetLanguage(*die.GetCU());
3183 TypeSystemSP type_system = nullptr;
3184 if (language != eLanguageTypeUnknown) {
3185 auto type_system_or_err = GetTypeSystemForLanguage(language);
3186 if (auto err = type_system_or_err.takeError()) {
3187 LLDB_LOG_ERROR(GetLog(LLDBLog::Symbols), std::move(err),
3188 "Cannot get TypeSystem for language {1}: {0}",
3189 Language::GetNameForLanguageType(language));
3190 } else {
3191 type_system = *type_system_or_err;
3192 }
3193 }
3194
3195 // See comments below about -gsimple-template-names for why we attempt to
3196 // compute missing template parameter names.
3197 std::vector<std::string> template_params;
3198 DWARFDeclContext die_dwarf_decl_ctx;
3199 DWARFASTParser *dwarf_ast =
3200 type_system ? type_system->GetDWARFParser() : nullptr;
3201 for (DWARFDIE ctx_die = die; ctx_die && !isUnitType(ctx_die.Tag());
3202 ctx_die = ctx_die.GetParentDeclContextDIE()) {
3203 die_dwarf_decl_ctx.AppendDeclContext(ctx_die.Tag(), ctx_die.GetName());
3204 template_params.push_back(
3205 (ctx_die.IsStructUnionOrClass() && dwarf_ast)
3206 ? dwarf_ast->GetDIEClassTemplateParams(ctx_die)
3207 : "");
3208 }
3209 const bool any_template_params = llvm::any_of(
3210 template_params, [](llvm::StringRef p) { return !p.empty(); });
3211
3212 auto die_matches = [&](DWARFDIE type_die) {
3213 // Resolve the type if both have the same tag or {class, struct} tags.
3214 const bool tag_matches =
3215 type_die.Tag() == tag ||
3216 (IsStructOrClassTag(type_die.Tag()) && IsStructOrClassTag(tag));
3217 if (!tag_matches)
3218 return false;
3219 if (any_template_params) {
3220 size_t pos = 0;
3221 for (DWARFDIE ctx_die = type_die; ctx_die && !isUnitType(ctx_die.Tag()) &&
3222 pos < template_params.size();
3223 ctx_die = ctx_die.GetParentDeclContextDIE(), ++pos) {
3224 if (template_params[pos].empty())
3225 continue;
3226 if (template_params[pos] !=
3227 dwarf_ast->GetDIEClassTemplateParams(ctx_die))
3228 return false;
3229 }
3230 if (pos != template_params.size())
3231 return false;
3232 }
3233 return true;
3234 };
3235 DWARFDIE result;
3236 m_index->GetFullyQualifiedType(die_dwarf_decl_ctx, [&](DWARFDIE type_die) {
3237 // Make sure type_die's language matches the type system we are
3238 // looking for. We don't want to find a "Foo" type from Java if we
3239 // are looking for a "Foo" type for C, C++, ObjC, or ObjC++.
3240 if (type_system &&
3241 !type_system->SupportsLanguage(GetLanguage(*type_die.GetCU())))
3242 return IterationAction::Continue;
3243
3244 if (!die_matches(type_die)) {
3245 if (log) {
3246 GetObjectFile()->GetModule()->LogMessage(
3247 log,
3248 "SymbolFileDWARF::FindDefinitionDIE(tag={0} ({1}), "
3249 "name='{2}') ignoring die={3:x16} ({4})",
3250 DW_TAG_value_to_name(tag), tag, name, type_die.GetOffset(),
3251 type_die.GetName());
3252 }
3253 return IterationAction::Continue;
3254 }
3255
3256 if (log) {
3257 DWARFDeclContext type_dwarf_decl_ctx = type_die.GetDWARFDeclContext();
3258 GetObjectFile()->GetModule()->LogMessage(
3259 log,
3260 "SymbolFileDWARF::FindDefinitionTypeDIE(tag={0} ({1}), name='{2}') "
3261 "trying die={3:x16} ({4})",
3262 DW_TAG_value_to_name(tag), tag, name, type_die.GetOffset(),
3263 type_dwarf_decl_ctx.GetQualifiedName());
3264 }
3265
3266 result = type_die;
3267 return IterationAction::Stop;
3268 });
3269 return result;
3270}
3271
3272TypeSP SymbolFileDWARF::ParseType(const SymbolContext &sc, const DWARFDIE &die,
3273 bool *type_is_new_ptr) {
3274 if (!die)
3275 return {};
3276
3277 auto type_system_or_err = GetTypeSystemForLanguage(GetLanguage(*die.GetCU()));
3278 if (auto err = type_system_or_err.takeError()) {
3279 LLDB_LOG_ERROR(GetLog(LLDBLog::Symbols), std::move(err),
3280 "Unable to parse type: {0}");
3281 return {};
3282 }
3283 auto ts = *type_system_or_err;
3284 if (!ts)
3285 return {};
3286
3287 DWARFASTParser *dwarf_ast = ts->GetDWARFParser();
3288 if (!dwarf_ast)
3289 return {};
3290
3291 TypeSP type_sp = dwarf_ast->ParseTypeFromDWARF(sc, die, type_is_new_ptr);
3292 if (type_sp) {
3293 if (die.Tag() == DW_TAG_subprogram) {
3294 std::string scope_qualified_name(GetDeclContextForUID(die.GetID())
3295 .GetScopeQualifiedName()
3296 .AsCString(""));
3297 if (scope_qualified_name.size()) {
3298 m_function_scope_qualified_name_map[scope_qualified_name].insert(
3299 *die.GetDIERef());
3300 }
3301 }
3302 }
3303
3304 return type_sp;
3305}
3306
3307size_t SymbolFileDWARF::ParseTypes(const SymbolContext &sc,
3308 const DWARFDIE &orig_die,
3309 bool parse_siblings, bool parse_children) {
3310 size_t types_added = 0;
3311 DWARFDIE die = orig_die;
3312
3313 while (die) {
3314 const dw_tag_t tag = die.Tag();
3315 bool type_is_new = false;
3316
3317 Tag dwarf_tag = static_cast<Tag>(tag);
3318
3319 // TODO: Currently ParseTypeFromDWARF(...) which is called by ParseType(...)
3320 // does not handle DW_TAG_subrange_type. It is not clear if this is a bug or
3321 // not.
3322 if (isType(dwarf_tag) && tag != DW_TAG_subrange_type)
3323 ParseType(sc, die, &type_is_new);
3324
3325 if (type_is_new)
3326 ++types_added;
3327
3328 if (parse_children && die.HasChildren()) {
3329 if (die.Tag() == DW_TAG_subprogram) {
3330 SymbolContext child_sc(sc);
3331 child_sc.function = sc.comp_unit->FindFunctionByUID(die.GetID()).get();
3332 types_added += ParseTypes(child_sc, die.GetFirstChild(), true, true);
3333 } else
3334 types_added += ParseTypes(sc, die.GetFirstChild(), true, true);
3335 }
3336
3337 if (parse_siblings)
3338 die = die.GetSibling();
3339 else
3340 die.Clear();
3341 }
3342 return types_added;
3343}
3344
3345size_t SymbolFileDWARF::ParseBlocksRecursive(Function &func) {
3346 std::lock_guard<std::recursive_mutex> guard(GetModuleMutex());
3347 CompileUnit *comp_unit = func.GetCompileUnit();
3348 lldbassert(comp_unit);
3349
3350 DWARFUnit *dwarf_cu = GetDWARFCompileUnit(comp_unit);
3351 if (!dwarf_cu)
3352 return 0;
3353
3354 size_t functions_added = 0;
3355 const dw_offset_t function_die_offset = DIERef(func.GetID()).die_offset();
3356 DWARFDIE function_die =
3357 dwarf_cu->GetNonSkeletonUnit().GetDIE(function_die_offset);
3358 if (function_die) {
3359 // We can't use the file address from the Function object as (in the OSO
3360 // case) it will already be remapped to the main module.
3361 if (llvm::Expected<llvm::DWARFAddressRangesVector> ranges =
3362 function_die.GetDIE()->GetAttributeAddressRanges(
3363 function_die.GetCU(),
3364 /*check_hi_lo_pc=*/true)) {
3365 if (ranges->empty())
3366 return 0;
3367 dw_addr_t function_file_addr = ranges->begin()->LowPC;
3368 if (function_file_addr != LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS)
3369 ParseBlocksRecursive(*comp_unit, &func.GetBlock(false),
3370 function_die.GetFirstChild(), function_file_addr);
3371 } else {
3372 LLDB_LOG_ERROR(GetLog(DWARFLog::DebugInfo), ranges.takeError(),
3373 "{1:x}: {0}", dwarf_cu->GetOffset());
3374 }
3375 }
3376
3377 return functions_added;
3378}
3379
3380size_t SymbolFileDWARF::ParseTypes(CompileUnit &comp_unit) {
3381 std::lock_guard<std::recursive_mutex> guard(GetModuleMutex());
3382 size_t types_added = 0;
3383 DWARFUnit *dwarf_cu = GetDWARFCompileUnit(&comp_unit);
3384 if (dwarf_cu) {
3385 DWARFDIE dwarf_cu_die = dwarf_cu->DIE();
3386 if (dwarf_cu_die && dwarf_cu_die.HasChildren()) {
3387 SymbolContext sc;
3388 sc.comp_unit = &comp_unit;
3389 types_added = ParseTypes(sc, dwarf_cu_die.GetFirstChild(), true, true);
3390 }
3391 }
3392
3393 return types_added;
3394}
3395
3396size_t SymbolFileDWARF::ParseVariablesForContext(const SymbolContext &sc) {
3397 std::lock_guard<std::recursive_mutex> guard(GetModuleMutex());
3398 if (sc.comp_unit != nullptr) {
3399 if (sc.function) {
3400 DWARFDIE function_die = GetDIE(sc.function->GetID());
3401
3402 dw_addr_t func_lo_pc = LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS;
3403 if (llvm::Expected<llvm::DWARFAddressRangesVector> ranges =
3404 function_die.GetDIE()->GetAttributeAddressRanges(
3405 function_die.GetCU(), /*check_hi_lo_pc=*/true)) {
3406 if (!ranges->empty())
3407 func_lo_pc = ranges->begin()->LowPC;
3408 } else {
3409 LLDB_LOG_ERROR(GetLog(DWARFLog::DebugInfo), ranges.takeError(),
3410 "DIE({1:x}): {0}", function_die.GetID());
3411 }
3412 if (func_lo_pc != LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS) {
3413 const size_t num_variables =
3414 ParseVariablesInFunctionContext(sc, function_die, func_lo_pc);
3415
3416 // Let all blocks know they have parse all their variables
3417 sc.function->GetBlock(false).SetDidParseVariables(true, true);
3418 return num_variables;
3419 }
3420 } else if (sc.comp_unit) {
3421 DWARFUnit *dwarf_cu = DebugInfo().GetUnitAtIndex(sc.comp_unit->GetID());
3422
3423 if (dwarf_cu == nullptr)
3424 return 0;
3425
3426 uint32_t vars_added = 0;
3427 VariableListSP variables(sc.comp_unit->GetVariableList(false));
3428
3429 if (variables.get() == nullptr) {
3430 variables = std::make_shared<VariableList>();
3431 sc.comp_unit->SetVariableList(variables);
3432
3433 m_index->GetGlobalVariables(*dwarf_cu, [&](DWARFDIE die) {
3434 VariableSP var_sp(ParseVariableDIECached(sc, die));
3435 if (var_sp) {
3436 variables->AddVariableIfUnique(var_sp);
3437 ++vars_added;
3438 }
3439 return IterationAction::Continue;
3440 });
3441 }
3442 return vars_added;
3443 }
3444 }
3445 return 0;
3446}
3447
3448VariableSP SymbolFileDWARF::ParseVariableDIECached(const SymbolContext &sc,
3449 const DWARFDIE &die) {
3450 if (!die)
3451 return nullptr;
3452
3453 DIEToVariableSP &die_to_variable = die.GetDWARF()->GetDIEToVariable();
3454
3455 VariableSP var_sp = die_to_variable[die.GetDIE()];
3456 if (var_sp)
3457 return var_sp;
3458
3459 var_sp = ParseVariableDIE(sc, die, LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS);
3460 if (var_sp) {
3461 die_to_variable[die.GetDIE()] = var_sp;
3462 if (DWARFDIE spec_die = die.GetReferencedDIE(DW_AT_specification))
3463 die_to_variable[spec_die.GetDIE()] = var_sp;
3464 }
3465 return var_sp;
3466}
3467
3468/// Creates a DWARFExpressionList from an DW_AT_location form_value.
3469static DWARFExpressionList GetExprListFromAtLocation(DWARFFormValue form_value,
3470 ModuleSP module,
3471 const DWARFDIE &die,
3472 const addr_t func_low_pc) {
3473 if (DWARFFormValue::IsBlockForm(form_value.Form())) {
3474 const DWARFDataExtractor &data = die.GetData();
3475
3476 uint64_t block_offset = form_value.BlockData() - data.GetDataStart();
3477 uint64_t block_length = form_value.Unsigned();
3478 return DWARFExpressionList(
3479 module, DataExtractor(data, block_offset, block_length), die.GetCU());
3480 }
3481
3482 DWARFExpressionList location_list(module, DWARFExpression(), die.GetCU());
3483 DataExtractor data = die.GetCU()->GetLocationData();
3484 dw_offset_t offset = form_value.Unsigned();
3485 if (form_value.Form() == DW_FORM_loclistx)
3486 offset = die.GetCU()->GetLoclistOffset(offset).value_or(-1);
3487 if (data.ValidOffset(offset)) {
3488 data = DataExtractor(data, offset, data.GetByteSize() - offset);
3489 const DWARFUnit *dwarf_cu = form_value.GetUnit();
3490 if (dwarf_cu->ParseDWARFLocationList(data, location_list))
3491 location_list.SetFuncFileAddress(func_low_pc);
3492 }
3493
3494 return location_list;
3495}
3496
3497/// Creates a DWARFExpressionList from an DW_AT_const_value. This is either a
3498/// block form, or a string, or a data form. For data forms, this returns an
3499/// empty list, as we cannot initialize it properly without a SymbolFileType.
3500static DWARFExpressionList
3501GetExprListFromAtConstValue(DWARFFormValue form_value, ModuleSP module,
3502 const DWARFDIE &die) {
3503 const DWARFDataExtractor &debug_info_data = die.GetData();
3504 if (DWARFFormValue::IsBlockForm(form_value.Form())) {
3505 // Retrieve the value as a block expression.
3506 uint64_t block_offset =
3507 form_value.BlockData() - debug_info_data.GetDataStart();
3508 uint64_t block_length = form_value.Unsigned();
3509 return DWARFExpressionList(
3510 module, DataExtractor(debug_info_data, block_offset, block_length),
3511 die.GetCU());
3512 }
3513 if (const char *str = form_value.AsCString())
3514 return DWARFExpressionList(module,
3515 DataExtractor(str, strlen(str) + 1,
3516 die.GetCU()->GetByteOrder(),
3517 die.GetCU()->GetAddressByteSize()),
3518 die.GetCU());
3519 return DWARFExpressionList(module, DWARFExpression(), die.GetCU());
3520}
3521
3522/// Global variables that are not initialized may have their address set to
3523/// zero. Since multiple variables may have this address, we cannot apply the
3524/// OSO relink address approach we normally use.
3525/// However, the executable will have a matching symbol with a good address;
3526/// this function attempts to find the correct address by looking into the
3527/// executable's symbol table. If it succeeds, the expr_list is updated with
3528/// the new address and the executable's symbol is returned.
3529static Symbol *fixupExternalAddrZeroVariable(
3530 SymbolFileDWARFDebugMap &debug_map_symfile, llvm::StringRef name,
3531 DWARFExpressionList &expr_list, const DWARFDIE &die) {
3532 ObjectFile *debug_map_objfile = debug_map_symfile.GetObjectFile();
3533 if (!debug_map_objfile)
3534 return nullptr;
3535
3536 Symtab *debug_map_symtab = debug_map_objfile->GetSymtab();
3537 if (!debug_map_symtab)
3538 return nullptr;
3539 Symbol *exe_symbol = debug_map_symtab->FindFirstSymbolWithNameAndType(
3540 ConstString(name), eSymbolTypeData, Symtab::eDebugYes,
3541 Symtab::eVisibilityExtern);
3542 if (!exe_symbol || !exe_symbol->ValueIsAddress())
3543 return nullptr;
3544 const addr_t exe_file_addr = exe_symbol->GetAddressRef().GetFileAddress();
3545 if (exe_file_addr == LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS)
3546 return nullptr;
3547
3548 DWARFExpression *location = expr_list.GetMutableExpressionAtAddress();
3549 if (location->Update_DW_OP_addr(die.GetCU(), exe_file_addr))
3550 return exe_symbol;
3551 return nullptr;
3552}
3553
3554VariableSP SymbolFileDWARF::ParseVariableDIE(const SymbolContext &sc,
3555 const DWARFDIE &die,
3556 const lldb::addr_t func_low_pc) {
3557 if (die.GetDWARF() != this)
3558 return die.GetDWARF()->ParseVariableDIE(sc, die, func_low_pc);
3559
3560 if (!die)
3561 return nullptr;
3562
3563 const dw_tag_t tag = die.Tag();
3564 ModuleSP module = GetObjectFile()->GetModule();
3565
3566 if (tag != DW_TAG_variable && tag != DW_TAG_constant &&
3567 tag != DW_TAG_member && (tag != DW_TAG_formal_parameter || !sc.function))
3568 return nullptr;
3569
3570 DWARFAttributes attributes = die.GetAttributes();
3571 const char *name = nullptr;
3572 const char *mangled = nullptr;
3573 Declaration decl;
3574 DWARFFormValue type_die_form;
3575 bool is_external = false;
3576 bool is_artificial = false;
3577 std::optional<uint64_t> tag_offset = std::nullopt;
3578 DWARFFormValue const_value_form, location_form;
3579 Variable::RangeList scope_ranges;
3580
3581 for (size_t i = 0; i < attributes.Size(); ++i) {
3582 dw_attr_t attr = attributes.AttributeAtIndex(i);
3583 DWARFFormValue form_value;
3584
3585 if (!attributes.ExtractFormValueAtIndex(i, form_value))
3586 continue;
3587 switch (attr) {
3588 case DW_AT_LLVM_tag_offset:
3589 tag_offset = form_value.Unsigned();
3590 break;
3591 case DW_AT_decl_file:
3592 decl.SetFile(
3593 attributes.CompileUnitAtIndex(i)->GetFile(form_value.Unsigned()));
3594 break;
3595 case DW_AT_decl_line:
3596 decl.SetLine(form_value.Unsigned());
3597 break;
3598 case DW_AT_decl_column:
3599 decl.SetColumn(form_value.Unsigned());
3600 break;
3601 case DW_AT_name:
3602 name = form_value.AsCString();
3603 break;
3604 case DW_AT_linkage_name:
3605 case DW_AT_MIPS_linkage_name:
3606 mangled = form_value.AsCString();
3607 break;
3608 case DW_AT_type:
3609 // DW_AT_type on declaration may be less accurate than
3610 // that of definition, so don't overwrite it.
3611 if (!type_die_form.IsValid())
3612 type_die_form = form_value;
3613 break;
3614 case DW_AT_external:
3615 is_external = form_value.Boolean();
3616 break;
3617 case DW_AT_const_value:
3618 const_value_form = form_value;
3619 break;
3620 case DW_AT_location:
3621 location_form = form_value;
3622 break;
3623 case DW_AT_start_scope:
3624 // TODO: Implement this.
3625 break;
3626 case DW_AT_artificial:
3627 is_artificial = form_value.Boolean();
3628 break;
3629 case DW_AT_declaration:
3630 case DW_AT_description:
3631 case DW_AT_endianity:
3632 case DW_AT_segment:
3633 case DW_AT_specification:
3634 case DW_AT_visibility:
3635 default:
3636 case DW_AT_abstract_origin:
3637 case DW_AT_sibling:
3638 break;
3639 }
3640 }
3641
3642 // Prefer DW_AT_location over DW_AT_const_value. Both can be emitted e.g.
3643 // for static constexpr member variables -- DW_AT_const_value and
3644 // DW_AT_location will both be present in the DIE defining the member.
3645 bool location_is_const_value_data =
3646 const_value_form.IsValid() && !location_form.IsValid();
3647
3648 DWARFExpressionList location_list = [&] {
3649 if (location_form.IsValid())
3650 return GetExprListFromAtLocation(location_form, module, die, func_low_pc);
3651 if (const_value_form.IsValid())
3652 return GetExprListFromAtConstValue(const_value_form, module, die);
3653 return DWARFExpressionList(module, DWARFExpression(), die.GetCU());
3654 }();
3655
3656 const DWARFDIE parent_context_die = GetDeclContextDIEContainingDIE(die);
3657 const DWARFDIE sc_parent_die = GetParentSymbolContextDIE(die);
3658 const dw_tag_t parent_tag = sc_parent_die.Tag();
3659 bool is_static_member = (parent_tag == DW_TAG_compile_unit ||
3660 parent_tag == DW_TAG_partial_unit) &&
3661 (parent_context_die.Tag() == DW_TAG_class_type ||
3662 parent_context_die.Tag() == DW_TAG_structure_type);
3663
3664 ValueType scope = eValueTypeInvalid;
3665 SymbolContextScope *symbol_context_scope = nullptr;
3666
3667 bool has_explicit_mangled = mangled != nullptr;
3668 if (!mangled) {
3669 // LLDB relies on the mangled name (DW_TAG_linkage_name or
3670 // DW_AT_MIPS_linkage_name) to generate fully qualified names
3671 // of global variables with commands like "frame var j". For
3672 // example, if j were an int variable holding a value 4 and
3673 // declared in a namespace B which in turn is contained in a
3674 // namespace A, the command "frame var j" returns
3675 // "(int) A::B::j = 4".
3676 // If the compiler does not emit a linkage name, we should be
3677 // able to generate a fully qualified name from the
3678 // declaration context.
3679 if ((parent_tag == DW_TAG_compile_unit ||
3680 parent_tag == DW_TAG_partial_unit) &&
3681 Language::LanguageIsCPlusPlus(GetLanguage(*die.GetCU())))
3682 mangled = die.GetDWARFDeclContext()
3683 .GetQualifiedNameAsConstString()
3684 .GetCString();
3685 }
3686
3687 if (tag == DW_TAG_formal_parameter)
3688 scope = eValueTypeVariableArgument;
3689 else {
3690 // DWARF doesn't specify if a DW_TAG_variable is a local, global
3691 // or static variable, so we have to do a little digging:
3692 // 1) DW_AT_linkage_name implies static lifetime (but may be missing)
3693 // 2) An empty DW_AT_location is an (optimized-out) static lifetime var.
3694 // 3) DW_AT_location containing a DW_OP_addr implies static lifetime.
3695 // Clang likes to combine small global variables into the same symbol
3696 // with locations like: DW_OP_addr(0x1000), DW_OP_constu(2), DW_OP_plus
3697 // so we need to look through the whole expression.
3698 bool has_explicit_location = location_form.IsValid();
3699 bool is_static_lifetime =
3700 has_explicit_mangled ||
3701 (has_explicit_location && !location_list.IsValid());
3702 // Check if the location has a DW_OP_addr with any address value...
3703 lldb::addr_t location_DW_OP_addr = LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS;
3704 if (!location_is_const_value_data) {
3705 if (const DWARFExpression *location =
3706 location_list.GetAlwaysValidExpr()) {
3707 if (auto maybe_location_DW_OP_addr =
3708 location->GetLocation_DW_OP_addr(location_form.GetUnit())) {
3709 location_DW_OP_addr = *maybe_location_DW_OP_addr;
3710 } else {
3711 StreamString strm;
3712 location->DumpLocation(&strm, eDescriptionLevelFull, nullptr);
3713 GetObjectFile()->GetModule()->ReportError(
3714 "{0:x16}: {1} ({2}) has an invalid location: {3}: {4}",
3715 die.GetOffset(), DW_TAG_value_to_name(die.Tag()), die.Tag(),
3716 llvm::fmt_consume(maybe_location_DW_OP_addr.takeError()),
3717 strm.GetData());
3718 }
3719 }
3720 if (location_DW_OP_addr != LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS)
3721 is_static_lifetime = true;
3722 }
3723 SymbolFileDWARFDebugMap *debug_map_symfile = GetDebugMapSymfile();
3724 if (debug_map_symfile)
3725 // Set the module of the expression to the linked module
3726 // instead of the object file so the relocated address can be
3727 // found there.
3728 location_list.SetModule(debug_map_symfile->GetObjectFile()->GetModule());
3729
3730 if (is_static_lifetime) {
3731 if (is_external)
3732 scope = eValueTypeVariableGlobal;
3733 else
3734 scope = eValueTypeVariableStatic;
3735
3736 if (debug_map_symfile) {
3737 bool linked_oso_file_addr = false;
3738
3739 if (is_external && location_DW_OP_addr == 0) {
3740 if (Symbol *exe_symbol = fixupExternalAddrZeroVariable(
3741 *debug_map_symfile, mangled ? mangled : name, location_list,
3742 die)) {
3743 linked_oso_file_addr = true;
3744 symbol_context_scope = exe_symbol;
3745 }
3746 }
3747
3748 if (!linked_oso_file_addr) {
3749 // The DW_OP_addr is not zero, but it contains a .o file address
3750 // which needs to be linked up correctly.
3751 const lldb::addr_t exe_file_addr =
3752 debug_map_symfile->LinkOSOFileAddress(this, location_DW_OP_addr);
3753 if (exe_file_addr != LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS) {
3754 // Update the file address for this variable
3755 DWARFExpression *location =
3756 location_list.GetMutableExpressionAtAddress();
3757 location->Update_DW_OP_addr(die.GetCU(), exe_file_addr);
3758 } else {
3759 // Variable didn't make it into the final executable
3760 return nullptr;
3761 }
3762 }
3763 }
3764 } else {
3765 if (location_is_const_value_data &&
3766 die.GetDIE()->IsGlobalOrStaticScopeVariable())
3767 scope = eValueTypeVariableStatic;
3768 else {
3769 scope = eValueTypeVariableLocal;
3770 if (debug_map_symfile) {
3771 // We need to check for TLS addresses that we need to fixup
3772 if (location_list.ContainsThreadLocalStorage()) {
3773 location_list.LinkThreadLocalStorage(
3774 debug_map_symfile->GetObjectFile()->GetModule(),
3775 [this, debug_map_symfile](
3776 lldb::addr_t unlinked_file_addr) -> lldb::addr_t {
3777 return debug_map_symfile->LinkOSOFileAddress(
3778 this, unlinked_file_addr);
3779 });
3780 scope = eValueTypeVariableThreadLocal;
3781 }
3782 }
3783 }
3784 }
3785 }
3786
3787 if (symbol_context_scope == nullptr) {
3788 switch (parent_tag) {
3789 case DW_TAG_subprogram:
3790 case DW_TAG_inlined_subroutine:
3791 case DW_TAG_lexical_block:
3792 if (sc.function) {
3793 symbol_context_scope =
3794 sc.function->GetBlock(true).FindBlockByID(sc_parent_die.GetID());
3795 if (symbol_context_scope == nullptr)
3796 symbol_context_scope = sc.function;
3797 }
3798 break;
3799
3800 default:
3801 symbol_context_scope = sc.comp_unit;
3802 break;
3803 }
3804 }
3805
3806 if (!symbol_context_scope) {
3807 // Not ready to parse this variable yet. It might be a global or static
3808 // variable that is in a function scope and the function in the symbol
3809 // context wasn't filled in yet
3810 return nullptr;
3811 }
3812
3813 auto type_sp = std::make_shared<SymbolFileType>(
3814 *this, type_die_form.Reference().GetID());
3815
3816 bool use_type_size_for_value =
3817 location_is_const_value_data &&
3818 DWARFFormValue::IsDataForm(const_value_form.Form());
3819 if (use_type_size_for_value && type_sp->GetType()) {
3820 DWARFExpression *location = location_list.GetMutableExpressionAtAddress();
3821 location->UpdateValue(
3822 const_value_form.Unsigned(),
3823 llvm::expectedToOptional(type_sp->GetType()->GetByteSize(nullptr))
3824 .value_or(0),
3825 die.GetCU()->GetAddressByteSize());
3826 }
3827
3828 return std::make_shared<Variable>(
3829 die.GetID(), name, mangled, type_sp, scope, symbol_context_scope,
3830 scope_ranges, &decl, location_list, is_external, is_artificial,
3831 location_is_const_value_data, is_static_member, tag_offset);
3832}
3833
3834DWARFDIE
3835SymbolFileDWARF::FindBlockContainingSpecification(
3836 const DIERef &func_die_ref, dw_offset_t spec_block_die_offset) {
3837 // Give the concrete function die specified by "func_die_offset", find the
3838 // concrete block whose DW_AT_specification or DW_AT_abstract_origin points
3839 // to "spec_block_die_offset"
3840 return FindBlockContainingSpecification(GetDIE(func_die_ref),
3841 spec_block_die_offset);
3842}
3843
3844DWARFDIE
3845SymbolFileDWARF::FindBlockContainingSpecification(
3846 const DWARFDIE &die, dw_offset_t spec_block_die_offset) {
3847 if (die) {
3848 switch (die.Tag()) {
3849 case DW_TAG_subprogram:
3850 case DW_TAG_inlined_subroutine:
3851 case DW_TAG_lexical_block: {
3852 if (die.GetReferencedDIE(DW_AT_specification).GetOffset() ==
3853 spec_block_die_offset)
3854 return die;
3855
3856 if (die.GetReferencedDIE(DW_AT_abstract_origin).GetOffset() ==
3857 spec_block_die_offset)
3858 return die;
3859 } break;
3860 default:
3861 break;
3862 }
3863
3864 // Give the concrete function die specified by "func_die_offset", find the
3865 // concrete block whose DW_AT_specification or DW_AT_abstract_origin points
3866 // to "spec_block_die_offset"
3867 for (DWARFDIE child_die : die.children()) {
3868 DWARFDIE result_die =
3869 FindBlockContainingSpecification(child_die, spec_block_die_offset);
3870 if (result_die)
3871 return result_die;
3872 }
3873 }
3874
3875 return DWARFDIE();
3876}
3877
3878void SymbolFileDWARF::ParseAndAppendGlobalVariable(
3879 const SymbolContext &sc, const DWARFDIE &die,
3880 VariableList &cc_variable_list) {
3881 if (!die)
3882 return;
3883
3884 dw_tag_t tag = die.Tag();
3885 if (tag != DW_TAG_variable && tag != DW_TAG_constant && tag != DW_TAG_member)
3886 return;
3887
3888 // Check to see if we have already parsed this variable or constant?
3889 VariableSP var_sp = GetDIEToVariable()[die.GetDIE()];
3890 if (var_sp) {
3891 cc_variable_list.AddVariableIfUnique(var_sp);
3892 return;
3893 }
3894
3895 // We haven't parsed the variable yet, lets do that now. Also, let us include
3896 // the variable in the relevant compilation unit's variable list, if it
3897 // exists.
3898 VariableListSP variable_list_sp;
3899 DWARFDIE sc_parent_die = GetParentSymbolContextDIE(die);
3900 dw_tag_t parent_tag = sc_parent_die.Tag();
3901 switch (parent_tag) {
3902 case DW_TAG_compile_unit:
3903 case DW_TAG_partial_unit:
3904 if (sc.comp_unit != nullptr) {
3905 variable_list_sp = sc.comp_unit->GetVariableList(false);
3906 } else {
3907 GetObjectFile()->GetModule()->ReportError(
3908 "parent {0:x8} {1} ({2}) with no valid compile unit in "
3909 "symbol context for {3:x8} {4} ({5}).\n",
3910 sc_parent_die.GetID(), DW_TAG_value_to_name(sc_parent_die.Tag()),
3911 sc_parent_die.Tag(), die.GetID(), DW_TAG_value_to_name(die.Tag()),
3912 die.Tag());
3913 return;
3914 }
3915 break;
3916
3917 default:
3918 LLDB_LOG(GetLog(DWARFLog::Lookups),
3919 "{0} '{1}' ({2:x8}) is not a global variable - ignoring", tag,
3920 die.GetName(), die.GetID());
3921 return;
3922 }
3923
3924 var_sp = ParseVariableDIECached(sc, die);
3925 if (!var_sp)
3926 return;
3927
3928 cc_variable_list.AddVariableIfUnique(var_sp);
3929 if (variable_list_sp)
3930 variable_list_sp->AddVariableIfUnique(var_sp);
3931}
3932
3933DIEArray
3934SymbolFileDWARF::MergeBlockAbstractParameters(const DWARFDIE &block_die,
3935 DIEArray &&variable_dies) {
3936 // DW_TAG_inline_subroutine objects may omit DW_TAG_formal_parameter in
3937 // instances of the function when they are unused (i.e., the parameter's
3938 // location list would be empty). The current DW_TAG_inline_subroutine may
3939 // refer to another DW_TAG_subprogram that might actually have the definitions
3940 // of the parameters and we need to include these so they show up in the
3941 // variables for this function (for example, in a stack trace). Let us try to
3942 // find the abstract subprogram that might contain the parameter definitions
3943 // and merge with the concrete parameters.
3944
3945 // Nothing to merge if the block is not an inlined function.
3946 if (block_die.Tag() != DW_TAG_inlined_subroutine) {
3947 return std::move(variable_dies);
3948 }
3949
3950 // Nothing to merge if the block does not have abstract parameters.
3951 DWARFDIE abs_die = block_die.GetReferencedDIE(DW_AT_abstract_origin);
3952 if (!abs_die || abs_die.Tag() != DW_TAG_subprogram ||
3953 !abs_die.HasChildren()) {
3954 return std::move(variable_dies);
3955 }
3956
3957 // For each abstract parameter, if we have its concrete counterpart, insert
3958 // it. Otherwise, insert the abstract parameter.
3959 DIEArray::iterator concrete_it = variable_dies.begin();
3960 DWARFDIE abstract_child = abs_die.GetFirstChild();
3961 DIEArray merged;
3962 bool did_merge_abstract = false;
3963 for (; abstract_child; abstract_child = abstract_child.GetSibling()) {
3964 if (abstract_child.Tag() == DW_TAG_formal_parameter) {
3965 if (concrete_it == variable_dies.end() ||
3966 GetDIE(*concrete_it).Tag() != DW_TAG_formal_parameter) {
3967 // We arrived at the end of the concrete parameter list, so all
3968 // the remaining abstract parameters must have been omitted.
3969 // Let us insert them to the merged list here.
3970 merged.push_back(*abstract_child.GetDIERef());
3971 did_merge_abstract = true;
3972 continue;
3973 }
3974
3975 DWARFDIE origin_of_concrete =
3976 GetDIE(*concrete_it).GetReferencedDIE(DW_AT_abstract_origin);
3977 if (origin_of_concrete == abstract_child) {
3978 // The current abstract parameter is the origin of the current
3979 // concrete parameter, just push the concrete parameter.
3980 merged.push_back(*concrete_it);
3981 ++concrete_it;
3982 } else {
3983 // Otherwise, the parameter must have been omitted from the concrete
3984 // function, so insert the abstract one.
3985 merged.push_back(*abstract_child.GetDIERef());
3986 did_merge_abstract = true;
3987 }
3988 }
3989 }
3990
3991 // Shortcut if no merging happened.
3992 if (!did_merge_abstract)
3993 return std::move(variable_dies);
3994
3995 // We inserted all the abstract parameters (or their concrete counterparts).
3996 // Let us insert all the remaining concrete variables to the merged list.
3997 // During the insertion, let us check there are no remaining concrete
3998 // formal parameters. If that's the case, then just bailout from the merge -
3999 // the variable list is malformed.
4000 for (; concrete_it != variable_dies.end(); ++concrete_it) {
4001 if (GetDIE(*concrete_it).Tag() == DW_TAG_formal_parameter) {
4002 return std::move(variable_dies);
4003 }
4004 merged.push_back(*concrete_it);
4005 }
4006 return merged;
4007}
4008
4009size_t SymbolFileDWARF::ParseVariablesInFunctionContext(
4010 const SymbolContext &sc, const DWARFDIE &die,
4011 const lldb::addr_t func_low_pc) {
4012 if (!die || !sc.function)
4013 return 0;
4014
4015 DIEArray dummy_block_variables; // The recursive call should not add anything
4016 // to this vector because |die| should be a
4017 // subprogram, so all variables will be added
4018 // to the subprogram's list.
4019 return ParseVariablesInFunctionContextRecursive(sc, die, func_low_pc,
4020 dummy_block_variables);
4021}
4022
4023// This method parses all the variables in the blocks in the subtree of |die|,
4024// and inserts them to the variable list for all the nested blocks.
4025// The uninserted variables for the current block are accumulated in
4026// |accumulator|.
4027size_t SymbolFileDWARF::ParseVariablesInFunctionContextRecursive(
4028 const lldb_private::SymbolContext &sc, const DWARFDIE &die,
4029 lldb::addr_t func_low_pc, DIEArray &accumulator) {
4030 size_t vars_added = 0;
4031 dw_tag_t tag = die.Tag();
4032
4033 if ((tag == DW_TAG_variable) || (tag == DW_TAG_constant) ||
4034 (tag == DW_TAG_formal_parameter)) {
4035 accumulator.push_back(*die.GetDIERef());
4036 }
4037
4038 switch (tag) {
4039 case DW_TAG_subprogram:
4040 case DW_TAG_inlined_subroutine:
4041 case DW_TAG_lexical_block: {
4042 // If we start a new block, compute a new block variable list and recurse.
4043 Block *block =
4044 sc.function->GetBlock(/*can_create=*/true).FindBlockByID(die.GetID());
4045 if (block == nullptr) {
4046 // This must be a specification or abstract origin with a
4047 // concrete block counterpart in the current function. We need
4048 // to find the concrete block so we can correctly add the
4049 // variable to it.
4050 const DWARFDIE concrete_block_die = FindBlockContainingSpecification(
4051 GetDIE(sc.function->GetID()), die.GetOffset());
4052 if (concrete_block_die)
4053 block = sc.function->GetBlock(/*can_create=*/true)
4054 .FindBlockByID(concrete_block_die.GetID());
4055 }
4056
4057 if (block == nullptr)
4058 return 0;
4059
4060 const bool can_create = false;
4061 VariableListSP block_variable_list_sp =
4062 block->GetBlockVariableList(can_create);
4063 if (block_variable_list_sp.get() == nullptr) {
4064 block_variable_list_sp = std::make_shared<VariableList>();
4065 block->SetVariableList(block_variable_list_sp);
4066 }
4067
4068 DIEArray block_variables;
4069 for (DWARFDIE child = die.GetFirstChild(); child;
4070 child = child.GetSibling()) {
4071 vars_added += ParseVariablesInFunctionContextRecursive(
4072 sc, child, func_low_pc, block_variables);
4073 }
4074 block_variables =
4075 MergeBlockAbstractParameters(die, std::move(block_variables));
4076 vars_added += PopulateBlockVariableList(*block_variable_list_sp, sc,
4077 block_variables, func_low_pc);
4078 break;
4079 }
4080
4081 default:
4082 // Recurse to children with the same variable accumulator.
4083 for (DWARFDIE child = die.GetFirstChild(); child;
4084 child = child.GetSibling()) {
4085 vars_added += ParseVariablesInFunctionContextRecursive(
4086 sc, child, func_low_pc, accumulator);
4087 }
4088 break;
4089 }
4090
4091 return vars_added;
4092}
4093
4094size_t SymbolFileDWARF::PopulateBlockVariableList(
4095 VariableList &variable_list, const lldb_private::SymbolContext &sc,
4096 llvm::ArrayRef<DIERef> variable_dies, lldb::addr_t func_low_pc) {
4097 // Parse the variable DIEs and insert them to the list.
4098 for (auto &die : variable_dies) {
4099 if (VariableSP var_sp = ParseVariableDIE(sc, GetDIE(die), func_low_pc)) {
4100 variable_list.AddVariableIfUnique(var_sp);
4101 }
4102 }
4103 return variable_dies.size();
4104}
4105
4106/// Collect call site parameters in a DW_TAG_call_site DIE.
4107static CallSiteParameterArray
4108CollectCallSiteParameters(ModuleSP module, DWARFDIE call_site_die) {
4109 CallSiteParameterArray parameters;
4110 for (DWARFDIE child : call_site_die.children()) {
4111 if (child.Tag() != DW_TAG_call_site_parameter &&
4112 child.Tag() != DW_TAG_GNU_call_site_parameter)
4113 continue;
4114
4115 std::optional<DWARFExpressionList> LocationInCallee;
4116 std::optional<DWARFExpressionList> LocationInCaller;
4117
4118 DWARFAttributes attributes = child.GetAttributes();
4119
4120 // Parse the location at index \p attr_index within this call site parameter
4121 // DIE, or return std::nullopt on failure.
4122 auto parse_simple_location =
4123 [&](int attr_index) -> std::optional<DWARFExpressionList> {
4124 DWARFFormValue form_value;
4125 if (!attributes.ExtractFormValueAtIndex(attr_index, form_value))
4126 return {};
4127 if (!DWARFFormValue::IsBlockForm(form_value.Form()))
4128 return {};
4129 auto data = child.GetData();
4130 uint64_t block_offset = form_value.BlockData() - data.GetDataStart();
4131 uint64_t block_length = form_value.Unsigned();
4132 return DWARFExpressionList(
4133 module, DataExtractor(data, block_offset, block_length),
4134 child.GetCU());
4135 };
4136
4137 for (size_t i = 0; i < attributes.Size(); ++i) {
4138 dw_attr_t attr = attributes.AttributeAtIndex(i);
4139 if (attr == DW_AT_location)
4140 LocationInCallee = parse_simple_location(i);
4141 if (attr == DW_AT_call_value || attr == DW_AT_GNU_call_site_value)
4142 LocationInCaller = parse_simple_location(i);
4143 }
4144
4145 if (LocationInCallee && LocationInCaller) {
4146 CallSiteParameter param = {*LocationInCallee, *LocationInCaller};
4147 parameters.push_back(param);
4148 }
4149 }
4150 return parameters;
4151}
4152
4153/// Collect call graph edges present in a function DIE.
4154std::vector<std::unique_ptr<lldb_private::CallEdge>>
4155SymbolFileDWARF::CollectCallEdges(ModuleSP module, DWARFDIE function_die) {
4156 // Check if the function has a supported call site-related attribute.
4157 // TODO: In the future it may be worthwhile to support call_all_source_calls.
4158 bool has_call_edges =
4159 function_die.GetAttributeValueAsUnsigned(DW_AT_call_all_calls, 0) ||
4160 function_die.GetAttributeValueAsUnsigned(DW_AT_GNU_all_call_sites, 0);
4161 if (!has_call_edges)
4162 return {};
4163
4164 Log *log = GetLog(LLDBLog::Step);
4165 LLDB_LOG(log, "CollectCallEdges: Found call site info in {0}",
4166 function_die.GetPubname());
4167
4168 // Scan the DIE for TAG_call_site entries.
4169 // TODO: A recursive scan of all blocks in the subprogram is needed in order
4170 // to be DWARF5-compliant. This may need to be done lazily to be performant.
4171 // For now, assume that all entries are nested directly under the subprogram
4172 // (this is the kind of DWARF LLVM produces) and parse them eagerly.
4173 std::vector<std::unique_ptr<CallEdge>> call_edges;
4174 for (DWARFDIE child : function_die.children()) {
4175 if (child.Tag() != DW_TAG_call_site && child.Tag() != DW_TAG_GNU_call_site)
4176 continue;
4177
4178 std::optional<DWARFDIE> call_origin;
4179 std::optional<DWARFExpressionList> call_target;
4180 addr_t return_pc = LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS;
4181 addr_t call_inst_pc = LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS;
4182 addr_t low_pc = LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS;
4183 bool tail_call = false;
4184
4185 // Second DW_AT_low_pc may come from DW_TAG_subprogram referenced by
4186 // DW_TAG_GNU_call_site's DW_AT_abstract_origin overwriting our 'low_pc'.
4187 // So do not inherit attributes from DW_AT_abstract_origin.
4188 DWARFAttributes attributes = child.GetAttributes(DWARFDIE::Recurse::no);
4189 for (size_t i = 0; i < attributes.Size(); ++i) {
4190 DWARFFormValue form_value;
4191 if (!attributes.ExtractFormValueAtIndex(i, form_value)) {
4192 LLDB_LOG(log, "CollectCallEdges: Could not extract TAG_call_site form");
4193 break;
4194 }
4195
4196 dw_attr_t attr = attributes.AttributeAtIndex(i);
4197
4198 if (attr == DW_AT_call_tail_call || attr == DW_AT_GNU_tail_call)
4199 tail_call = form_value.Boolean();
4200
4201 // Extract DW_AT_call_origin (the call target's DIE).
4202 if (attr == DW_AT_call_origin || attr == DW_AT_abstract_origin) {
4203 call_origin = form_value.Reference();
4204 if (!call_origin->IsValid()) {
4205 LLDB_LOG(log, "CollectCallEdges: Invalid call origin in {0}",
4206 function_die.GetPubname());
4207 break;
4208 }
4209 }
4210
4211 if (attr == DW_AT_low_pc)
4212 low_pc = form_value.Address();
4213
4214 // Extract DW_AT_call_return_pc (the PC the call returns to) if it's
4215 // available. It should only ever be unavailable for tail call edges, in
4216 // which case use LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS.
4217 if (attr == DW_AT_call_return_pc)
4218 return_pc = form_value.Address();
4219
4220 // Extract DW_AT_call_pc (the PC at the call/branch instruction). It
4221 // should only ever be unavailable for non-tail calls, in which case use
4222 // LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS.
4223 if (attr == DW_AT_call_pc)
4224 call_inst_pc = form_value.Address();
4225
4226 // Extract DW_AT_call_target (the location of the address of the indirect
4227 // call).
4228 if (attr == DW_AT_call_target || attr == DW_AT_GNU_call_site_target) {
4229 if (!DWARFFormValue::IsBlockForm(form_value.Form())) {
4230 LLDB_LOG(log,
4231 "CollectCallEdges: AT_call_target does not have block form");
4232 break;
4233 }
4234
4235 auto data = child.GetData();
4236 uint64_t block_offset = form_value.BlockData() - data.GetDataStart();
4237 uint64_t block_length = form_value.Unsigned();
4238 call_target = DWARFExpressionList(
4239 module, DataExtractor(data, block_offset, block_length),
4240 child.GetCU());
4241 }
4242 }
4243 if (!call_origin && !call_target) {
4244 LLDB_LOG(log, "CollectCallEdges: call site without any call target");
4245 continue;
4246 }
4247
4248 addr_t caller_address;
4249 CallEdge::AddrType caller_address_type;
4250 if (return_pc != LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS) {
4251 caller_address = return_pc;
4252 caller_address_type = CallEdge::AddrType::AfterCall;
4253 } else if (low_pc != LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS) {
4254 caller_address = low_pc;
4255 caller_address_type = CallEdge::AddrType::AfterCall;
4256 } else if (call_inst_pc != LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS) {
4257 caller_address = call_inst_pc;
4258 caller_address_type = CallEdge::AddrType::Call;
4259 } else {
4260 LLDB_LOG(log, "CollectCallEdges: No caller address");
4261 continue;
4262 }
4263 // Adjust any PC forms. It needs to be fixed up if the main executable
4264 // contains a debug map (i.e. pointers to object files), because we need a
4265 // file address relative to the executable's text section.
4266 caller_address = FixupAddress(caller_address);
4267
4268 // Extract call site parameters.
4269 CallSiteParameterArray parameters =
4270 CollectCallSiteParameters(module, child);
4271
4272 std::unique_ptr<CallEdge> edge;
4273 if (call_origin) {
4274 LLDB_LOG(log,
4275 "CollectCallEdges: Found call origin: {0} (retn-PC: {1:x}) "
4276 "(call-PC: {2:x})",
4277 call_origin->GetPubname(), return_pc, call_inst_pc);
4278 edge = std::make_unique<DirectCallEdge>(
4279 call_origin->GetMangledName(), caller_address_type, caller_address,
4280 tail_call, std::move(parameters));
4281 } else {
4282 if (log) {
4283 StreamString call_target_desc;
4284 call_target->GetDescription(&call_target_desc, eDescriptionLevelBrief,
4285 nullptr);
4286 LLDB_LOG(log, "CollectCallEdges: Found indirect call target: {0}",
4287 call_target_desc.GetString());
4288 }
4289 edge = std::make_unique<IndirectCallEdge>(
4290 *call_target, caller_address_type, caller_address, tail_call,
4291 std::move(parameters));
4292 }
4293
4294 if (log && parameters.size()) {
4295 for (const CallSiteParameter &param : parameters) {
4296 StreamString callee_loc_desc, caller_loc_desc;
4297 param.LocationInCallee.GetDescription(&callee_loc_desc,
4298 eDescriptionLevelBrief, nullptr);
4299 param.LocationInCaller.GetDescription(&caller_loc_desc,
4300 eDescriptionLevelBrief, nullptr);
4301 LLDB_LOG(log, "CollectCallEdges: \tparam: {0} => {1}",
4302 callee_loc_desc.GetString(), caller_loc_desc.GetString());
4303 }
4304 }
4305
4306 call_edges.push_back(std::move(edge));
4307 }
4308 return call_edges;
4309}
4310
4311std::vector<std::unique_ptr<lldb_private::CallEdge>>
4312SymbolFileDWARF::ParseCallEdgesInFunction(lldb_private::UserID func_id) {
4313 // ParseCallEdgesInFunction must be called at the behest of an exclusively
4314 // locked lldb::Function instance. Storage for parsed call edges is owned by
4315 // the lldb::Function instance: locking at the SymbolFile level would be too
4316 // late, because the act of storing results from ParseCallEdgesInFunction
4317 // would be racy.
4318 DWARFDIE func_die = GetDIE(func_id.GetID());
4319 if (func_die.IsValid())
4320 return CollectCallEdges(GetObjectFile()->GetModule(), func_die);
4321 return {};
4322}
4323
4324void SymbolFileDWARF::Dump(lldb_private::Stream &s) {
4325 SymbolFileCommon::Dump(s);
4326 m_index->Dump(s);
4327}
4328
4329void SymbolFileDWARF::DumpClangAST(Stream &s, llvm::StringRef filter,
4330 bool show_color) {
4331 auto ts_or_err = GetTypeSystemForLanguage(eLanguageTypeC_plus_plus);
4332 if (!ts_or_err)
4333 return;
4334 auto ts = *ts_or_err;
4335 TypeSystemClang *clang = llvm::dyn_cast_or_null<TypeSystemClang>(ts.get());
4336 if (!clang)
4337 return;
4338 clang->Dump(s.AsRawOstream(), filter, show_color);
4339}
4340
4341bool SymbolFileDWARF::GetSeparateDebugInfo(StructuredData::Dictionary &d,
4342 bool errors_only,
4343 bool load_all_debug_info) {
4344 StructuredData::Array separate_debug_info_files;
4345 DWARFDebugInfo &info = DebugInfo();
4346 const size_t num_cus = info.GetNumUnits();
4347 for (size_t cu_idx = 0; cu_idx < num_cus; cu_idx++) {
4348 DWARFUnit *unit = info.GetUnitAtIndex(cu_idx);
4349 DWARFCompileUnit *dwarf_cu = llvm::dyn_cast<DWARFCompileUnit>(unit);
4350 if (dwarf_cu == nullptr)
4351 continue;
4352
4353 // Check if this is a DWO unit by checking if it has a DWO ID.
4354 // NOTE: it seems that `DWARFUnit::IsDWOUnit` is always false?
4355 if (!dwarf_cu->GetDWOId().has_value())
4356 continue;
4357
4358 StructuredData::DictionarySP dwo_data =
4359 std::make_shared<StructuredData::Dictionary>();
4360 const uint64_t dwo_id = dwarf_cu->GetDWOId().value();
4361 dwo_data->AddIntegerItem("dwo_id", dwo_id);
4362
4363 if (const DWARFBaseDIE die = dwarf_cu->GetUnitDIEOnly()) {
4364 const char *dwo_name = GetDWOName(*dwarf_cu, *die.GetDIE());
4365 if (dwo_name) {
4366 dwo_data->AddStringItem("dwo_name", dwo_name);
4367 } else {
4368 dwo_data->AddStringItem("error", "missing dwo name");
4369 }
4370
4371 const char *comp_dir = die.GetDIE()->GetAttributeValueAsString(
4372 dwarf_cu, DW_AT_comp_dir, nullptr);
4373 if (comp_dir) {
4374 dwo_data->AddStringItem("comp_dir", comp_dir);
4375 }
4376 } else {
4377 dwo_data->AddStringItem(
4378 "error",
4379 llvm::formatv("unable to get unit DIE for DWARFUnit at {0:x}",
4380 dwarf_cu->GetOffset())
4381 .str());
4382 }
4383
4384 // If we have a DWO symbol file, that means we were able to successfully
4385 // load it.
4386 SymbolFile *dwo_symfile = dwarf_cu->GetDwoSymbolFile(load_all_debug_info);
4387 if (dwo_symfile) {
4388 dwo_data->AddStringItem(
4389 "resolved_dwo_path",
4390 dwo_symfile->GetObjectFile()->GetFileSpec().GetPath());
4391 } else {
4392 dwo_data->AddStringItem("error",
4393 dwarf_cu->GetDwoError().AsCString("unknown"));
4394 }
4395 dwo_data->AddBooleanItem("loaded", dwo_symfile != nullptr);
4396 if (!errors_only || dwo_data->HasKey("error"))
4397 separate_debug_info_files.AddItem(dwo_data);
4398 }
4399
4400 d.AddStringItem("type", "dwo");
4401 d.AddStringItem("symfile", GetMainObjectFile()->GetFileSpec().GetPath());
4402 d.AddItem("separate-debug-info-files",
4403 std::make_shared<StructuredData::Array>(
4404 std::move(separate_debug_info_files)));
4405 return true;
4406}
4407
4408SymbolFileDWARFDebugMap *SymbolFileDWARF::GetDebugMapSymfile() {
4409 if (m_debug_map_symfile == nullptr) {
4410 lldb::ModuleSP module_sp(m_debug_map_module_wp.lock());
4411 if (module_sp) {
4412 m_debug_map_symfile = llvm::cast<SymbolFileDWARFDebugMap>(
4413 module_sp->GetSymbolFile()->GetBackingSymbolFile());
4414 }
4415 }
4416 return m_debug_map_symfile;
4417}
4418
4419const std::shared_ptr<SymbolFileDWARFDwo> &SymbolFileDWARF::GetDwpSymbolFile() {
4420 llvm::call_once(m_dwp_symfile_once_flag, [this]() {
4421 if (m_objfile_sp->GetArchitecture().GetTriple().isAppleMachO())
4422 return;
4423
4424 // Create a list of files to try and append .dwp to.
4425 FileSpecList symfiles;
4426 // Append the module's object file path.
4427 const FileSpec module_fspec = m_objfile_sp->GetModule()->GetFileSpec();
4428 symfiles.Append(module_fspec);
4429 // Append the object file for this SymbolFile only if it is different from
4430 // the module's file path. Our main module could be "a.out", our symbol file
4431 // could be "a.debug" and our ".dwp" file might be "a.debug.dwp" instead of
4432 // "a.out.dwp".
4433 const FileSpec symfile_fspec(m_objfile_sp->GetFileSpec());
4434 if (symfile_fspec != module_fspec) {
4435 symfiles.Append(symfile_fspec);
4436 } else {
4437 // If we don't have a separate debug info file, then try stripping the
4438 // extension. The main module could be "a.debug" and the .dwp file could
4439 // be "a.dwp" instead of "a.debug.dwp".
4440 ConstString filename_no_ext =
4441 module_fspec.GetFileNameStrippingExtension();
4442 if (filename_no_ext != module_fspec.GetFilename()) {
4443 FileSpec module_spec_no_ext(module_fspec);
4444 module_spec_no_ext.SetFilename(filename_no_ext);
4445 symfiles.Append(module_spec_no_ext);
4446 }
4447 }
4448 Log *log = GetLog(DWARFLog::SplitDwarf);
4449 FileSpecList search_paths = Target::GetDefaultDebugFileSearchPaths();
4450 ModuleSpec module_spec;
4451 module_spec.GetFileSpec() = m_objfile_sp->GetFileSpec();
4452 FileSpec dwp_filespec;
4453 for (const auto &symfile : symfiles.files()) {
4454 module_spec.GetSymbolFileSpec() =
4455 FileSpec(symfile.GetPath() + ".dwp", symfile.GetPathStyle());
4456 LLDB_LOG(log, "Searching for DWP using: \"{0}\"",
4457 module_spec.GetSymbolFileSpec());
4458 dwp_filespec = PluginManager::LocateExecutableSymbolFile(
4459 module_spec, search_paths,
4460 m_objfile_sp->GetModule()->GetSymbolLocatorStatistics());
4461 if (FileSystem::Instance().Exists(dwp_filespec)) {
4462 break;
4463 }
4464 }
4465 if (!FileSystem::Instance().Exists(dwp_filespec)) {
4466 LLDB_LOG(log, "No DWP file found locally");
4467 // Fill in the UUID for the module we're trying to match for, so we can
4468 // find the correct DWP file, as the Debuginfod plugin uses *only* this
4469 // data to correctly match the DWP file with the binary.
4470 module_spec.GetUUID() = m_objfile_sp->GetUUID();
4471 dwp_filespec = PluginManager::LocateExecutableSymbolFile(
4472 module_spec, search_paths,
4473 m_objfile_sp->GetModule()->GetSymbolLocatorStatistics());
4474 }
4475 if (FileSystem::Instance().Exists(dwp_filespec)) {
4476 LLDB_LOG(log, "Found DWP file: \"{0}\"", dwp_filespec);
4477 DataExtractorSP dwp_file_extractor_sp;
4478 lldb::offset_t dwp_file_data_offset = 0;
4479 ObjectFileSP dwp_obj_file = ObjectFile::FindPlugin(
4480 GetObjectFile()->GetModule(), &dwp_filespec, 0,
4481 FileSystem::Instance().GetByteSize(dwp_filespec),
4482 dwp_file_extractor_sp, dwp_file_data_offset);
4483 if (dwp_obj_file) {
